chunks.text: It was a spring morning when I learned that my wife was pregnant.
Along with joy, a great responsibility settled on my shoulders.
Nine months later, our infant was born.
Our son.
Small hands, small feet, a peaceful sleeping face.
Just by being there, the world seemed to change.
But raising a child was far tougher than I had imagined.
Babies cry without regard to day nor night.
Frequently, we were chased by feeding and changing diapers.
Lack of sleep was severe.
Even when we desperately wanted sleep, the sound of his crying woke us immediately.
My wife and I were both exhausted.
And yet, the moment our son laughed, all the fatigue blew away.
The feel of his small hand gripping my finger.
When I feel the beat of his breast as he sleeps, I feel considerably happy.
Raising a child is also a process that makes a parent a parent.
While we seem to be raising the child
we are in truth being raised by the child.
Patience, endurance, unconditional love.
My son drew out of me qualities I had never possessed before.
A few years later, my son began attending nursery school.
Every morning, at parting, he waves his small hand.
The relationship between my son and me will walk through a long progression.
Until he is a teenager, until he is an adult
until one day he leaves home.
